response.justification: Strong geospatial domain expertise through GISCollective and proven ability to work across languages and complex systems provide a solid foundation. However, the lack of modern C++ commercial experience (last substantial C++ work was ~2015) is a significant concern for a role where C++ is the primary skill. Would require careful assessment of technical readiness and learning velocity.
strengths: Founded and maintains GISCollective, an open-source geospatial platform, demonstrating deep domain expertise in geospatial data processing
Proven distributed systems experience from Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests/second with microservices architecture
Demonstrated polyglot engineer who successfully learns new languages and problem domains across web, mobile, and systems contexts
concerns: Last substantial C++ work was 2014-2015 at National Instruments; recent 7+ years focused on JavaScript/TypeScript web stack
No Rust experience mentioned despite being listed as acceptable alternative to C++
Geospatial work focuses on data collection and visualization rather than high-performance routing pipeline optimization at scale
